results.agent_output:
Fixed the CUDA bounds bug in [src/vector_add.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_076_amp-deep3_561zvvfa/src/vector_add.cu#L20-L43) by making the launch cover the full array with ceiling division and guarding the kernel body with `if (i < N)`. That resolves both failure modes: skipped tail elements for sizes like `1000` or `1025`, and out-of-bounds access when the grid is rounded up.

Verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_vector_add.py` now passes with `7 passed`, including the non-multiple-size regression cases.

results.agent_output:
I fixed the race in the GPU histogram kernel by replacing the direct bin increment with `atomicAdd`, so concurrent threads no longer lose updates when they hit the same bin.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true src/histogram.cu -lcudadevrt -lm -o histogram` and running `./histogram` at sizes `100`, `10000`, and `100000`. All three runs reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `MAX_DIFF=0`, and matching CPU/GPU totals equal to `N`.

Fixed the off-by-one in [src/prefix_sum.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_079_amp-deep3_yinrbox_/src/prefix_sum.cu#L42-L46) by correcting the Blelloch down-sweep initialization. The kernel now writes the identity element `0.0f` into the last shared-memory slot before the down-sweep begins; previously it left the total sum there, which turns the result into an inclusive scan and shifts every output.

I verified it with a direct CUDA build using the requested flags and ran representative cases at sizes `16`, `128`, and `256`. All three reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `FIRST_OK=1`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the boundary bug in [src/conv1d.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_080_amp-deep3_oxd59taa/src/conv1d.cu#L29-L65) by changing the shared-memory halo loads to use block-relative indices instead of deriving the left halo from `gid - radius + lid`. The new logic computes `block_start` once, loads the left halo from `block_start + lid - radius`, loads the right halo from `block_start + blockDim.x + lid`, and zero-pads any out-of-range access as intended.

I verified it with a local `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` build and these sample runs all matched the CPU reference with zero boundary errors:
- `--size 500 --ksize 5 --seed 42`
- `--size 300 --ksize 11 --seed 99`
- `--size 200 --ksize 7 --seed 13`

All three reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `BOUNDARY_ERRORS=0`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the CUDA reduction bug in [src/reduction.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_081_amp-deep3_q04fqbz3/src/reduction.cu#L21-L57) by correcting both parts of the warp handoff.

The actual issues were:
1. The shuffle mask only enabled the low 16 lanes (`0x0000FFFF`), so half the warp never participated.
2. The shared-memory reduction stopped before the `s == 32` step, leaving 64 partial sums for a 32-lane warp shuffle to reduce.

The fix changes the mask to `0xFFFFFFFF` and extends the shared-memory loop to include `s >= WARP_SIZE`, so the final warp shuffle now receives exactly 32 fully accumulated values.

Verification:
- Compiled with `nvcc src/reduction.cu -o reduction_check -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`
- Ran:
  - `--size 10000 --seed 42` → `MATCH=1`
  - `--size 100000 --seed 99` → `MATCH=1`
  - `--size 7777 --seed 777` → `MATCH=1`
  - `--size 512 --seed 13` → `MATCH=1`

The reported relative errors are now all around `1e-8` to `1e-9`, well within tolerance.

Fixed the CSR kernel in [src/spmv_csr.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_082_amp-deep3_v5owir3r/src/spmv_csr.cu#L25-L39). The per-row accumulator now starts at `0.0f`, which makes empty rows correctly produce zero, and the row end is always taken from `row_ptr[row + 1]`, so the last row follows the same CSR bounds as every other row instead of using the incorrect `nnz - 1` special case.

Verification passed with both the existing test suite and a direct CUDA run:
- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_spmv_csr.py -q` → `7 passed`
- `nvcc src/spmv_csr.cu -o spmv_csr -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm && ./spmv_csr --size 200 --seed 42` → `MISMATCHES=0`, `EMPTY_ROW_ERRORS=0`, `MATCH=1`

Fixed the non-power-of-2 bitonic sort bug in [src/bitonic_sort.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_083_amp-deep3_k453o6xj/src/bitonic_sort.cu#L24-L89).

The two important changes are:
- The padding kernel now writes starting at `N`, so launches sized for `padded_N - N` actually fill the padded tail instead of missing it for cases like `300 -> 512` ([lines 24-29](file:///tmp/bench_task_083_amp-deep3_k453o6xj/src/bitonic_sort.cu#L24-L29)).
- The bitonic step now uses the standard `size`/`stride` network parameters, with swap direction derived from the active merge size via `(i & size) == 0`, which makes the ascending/descending stages correct and easier to reason about ([lines 31-54](file:///tmp/bench_task_083_amp-deep3_k453o6xj/src/bitonic_sort.cu#L31-L54), [lines 78-86](file:///tmp/bench_task_083_amp-deep3_k453o6xj/src/bitonic_sort.cu#L78-L86)).

Verification: `python3 -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed in 6.90s`.

Fixed the compaction bug in [src/stream_compact.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_2bshwr41/src/stream_compact.cu#L34-L105) by making the scan truly exclusive and keeping scatter/count logic aligned with that scan.

The key changes are in the [exclusive scan kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_2bshwr41/src/stream_compact.cu#L34-L53) and the [GPU driver](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_2bshwr41/src/stream_compact.cu#L68-L108):
- `scan_out[i]` is now the number of selected elements before `i`, so `scatter_kernel` writes each kept value to the correct slot with no off-by-one duplication/drop.
- The final count is now computed as `last_scan + last_flag`, which is the correct total for an exclusive scan.
- I also added a small `N <= 0` guard.

Verification:
- `nvcc src/stream_compact.cu -o stream_compact -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`
- `python3 -m pytest -q`

Result: `7 passed in 6.18s`

Fixed the signed-ordering bug in [src/radix_sort.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_085_amp-deep3_mz1mgn37/src/radix_sort.cu#L25-L67) by introducing a small `radix_key()` helper that XORs each `int` with `0x80000000u` before any bit extraction. That makes the radix passes compare signed integers in ascending order, so negatives sort before non-negatives while the stored values themselves remain unchanged.

Verification passed:
- `python3 -m pytest -q` → `7 passed`
- Direct CUDA runs after `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` all reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `SORTED=1`, and `NEG_AFTER_POS=0` for mixed signed inputs.

results.agent_output:
Updated [src/cg_reduce.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_087_amp-deep3_zh__edva/src/cg_reduce.cu#L22-L103) so the cooperative-groups reduction is tile-size correct instead of warp-size hardcoded. The kernel now reduces within each tile using `tile.size()` and `tile.thread_rank()` ([lines 32-45](file:///tmp/bench_task_087_amp-deep3_zh__edva/src/cg_reduce.cu#L32-L45)), then performs the second-stage aggregation with a shared-memory block reduction over all tile partials ([lines 48-67](file:///tmp/bench_task_087_amp-deep3_zh__edva/src/cg_reduce.cu#L48-L67)), which fixes the broken `4/8/16` paths and also handles `1` and `2`. I also added kernel dispatch cases for tile sizes `1` and `2` in the driver ([lines 82-101](file:///tmp/bench_task_087_amp-deep3_zh__edva/src/cg_reduce.cu#L82-L101)).

Verification passed with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`, direct runs for tile sizes `1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32` all returned `MATCH=1`, and `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_cg_reduce.py` passed with `7 passed`.

Fixed the unified-memory Jacobi loop in [src/jacobi_um.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_088_amp-deep3_zj5_77h3/src/jacobi_um.cu#L78-L95). The solver now prefetches `x0` as the read buffer and `x1` as the write buffer each iteration, launches the kernel as `jacobi_kernel(..., x0, x1, ...)`, then swaps the ping-pong pointers afterward. That restores correct Jacobi semantics instead of reading from the same buffer being overwritten. The final result buffer is still prefetched back to CPU before host access.

Verification passed with `python3 -m pytest tests/test_jacobi_um.py -q` (`6 passed`) and a direct CUDA build/run using `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`, which produced `MISMATCHES=0`, `MAX_ERROR=4.768372e-07`, and `MATCH=1` for `--size 64 --iters 20 --seed 42`.

results.agent_output:
Fixed the warp reduction bug in [src/warp_predicate.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_090_amp-deep3_y84e1hto/src/warp_predicate.cu#L48-L88).

The kernel now uses the live warp mask from `__activemask()` for both `__ballot_sync` calls and for the shuffle operations, so the tail warp is handled safely and the vote mask no longer truncates to 16 lanes. I also replaced the broken shuffle accumulation with a real in-place warp reduction guarded by the number of live lanes, which keeps mixed-predicate warps from folding in the wrong values during aggregation.

Verification: `python3 -m pytest -q` passed with `5 passed`, which exercises the CUDA build and the previously failing mixed-threshold cases.

Fixed the WMMA load bug in [src/wmma_gemm.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_091_amp-deep3_qyv7c3nb/src/wmma_gemm.cu#L41-L51): both input fragments now use `wmma::row_major`, and matrix A’s `wmma::load_matrix_sync` now uses leading dimension `K` instead of `N`. That matches the actual row-major storage of `A` and `B`, so the tensor core kernel now computes the same `C = A * B` as the CPU reference.

I verified it by compiling exactly as the bug report describes with `-arch=sm_70` and running three cases: `16x16x16`, `32x32x32`, and `64x64x32`. All three returned `MISMATCHES=0` and `MATCH=1`. The bundled `pytest` suite still errors during compilation because its harness omits the required `-arch=sm_70` flag; that’s a separate test configuration issue, and I didn’t change tests per your file-scope constraints.

results.agent_output:
Updated [src/dp_mergesort.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_dkg2v4it/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L6-L102) to remove the deadlocking device-side child-kernel pattern. The merge sort now recurses inline in [the device helper](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_dkg2v4it/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L58-L76) instead of launching children onto the shared default stream, and it uses an [insertion-sort cutoff of 32 elements](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_dkg2v4it/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L6-L7) so it stops recursing before the tiny-subarray overhead explodes.

Verified by compiling with `nvcc src/dp_mergesort.cu -o dp_mergesort_bin -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running the binary at sizes `500` and `4096`; both reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `SORTED=1`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the double-buffering bug in [src/tiled_gemm.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_095_amp-deep3_7lswl5q0/src/tiled_gemm.cu#L25-L74). The kernel now keeps separate `read_buf` and `write_buf` indices, computes from the currently loaded tile, then stages the next tile into the other shared-memory bank and synchronizes before swapping buffers. The critical change is in the loop at [lines 38-68](file:///tmp/bench_task_095_amp-deep3_7lswl5q0/src/tiled_gemm.cu#L38-L68), which removes the old overwrite of the active compute buffer.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running the same matrix sizes covered by the tests. All of these now report `MATCH=1`: `16x16x16`, `64x64x64`, `128x128x64`, `32x64x128`, `64x32x32`, and `256x256x256`. `pytest` itself wasn’t available in this environment, so verification was done by direct compile-and-run of the CUDA binary.

Fixed the CUDA BFS races in [src/bfs_warp.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_096_amp-deep3_73llters/src/bfs_warp.cu).

In [the BFS kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_096_amp-deep3_73llters/src/bfs_warp.cu#L38-L64), neighbor discovery now uses `atomicCAS(&dist[u], -1, next_level)` so each reachable vertex is claimed exactly once, and frontier insertion now uses `atomicAdd(out_size, 1)` so concurrent warps can’t overwrite each other’s queue slots. I also changed the neighbor loop to walk edges as `start + lane, start + lane + WARP_SIZE, ...`, which makes the CSR indexing explicitly relative to each frontier vertex’s adjacency range.

While verifying, I found a separate heap-corruption bug in [the random CSR generator](file:///tmp/bench_task_096_amp-deep3_73llters/src/bfs_warp.cu#L121-L156): it allocated `col_idx` from one set of degree counts and then rebuilt `row_ptr` from a different set. I replaced that with a stable two-pass generation using stored per-vertex degrees, so larger test cases now run reliably.

Verification: `python3 -m pytest -q` -> `7 passed in 6.07s`.

Fixed the CUDA FFT in [src/fft_ct.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L45-L140).

The core fixes were:
- Corrected bit-reversal to use the full `log2(N)` bits in [bit_reverse_kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L45-L60).
- Made the butterfly stage use explicit `span`/`half` semantics so partner indexing is `i + span/2`, not an ambiguous stride-derived offset, in [butterfly_kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L65-L92) and its launch loop in [gpu_fft](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L113-L123).
- Expressed the forward/inverse twiddle sign directly as `inverse ? + : -` in [the twiddle angle computation](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L77-L80).
- Fixed inverse normalization to divide by `N` in [the output scaling block](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_uvvll08a/src/fft_ct.cu#L125-L136).

Verification:
- `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_fft_ct.py` → `7 passed`
- `nvcc src/fft_ct.cu -o fft_ct_check -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` → compile succeeded

results.agent_output:
Fixed the CUDA traversal path in [src/ray_aabb.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_099_amp-deep3_c5vpqwd9/src/ray_aabb.cu#L83-L147) so it now matches the CPU reference behavior. The GPU ray-AABB test now initializes `tmax` to `FLT_MAX` and explicitly swaps `t1`/`t2` per axis when needed, which fixes negative-direction slab handling and wrong hit distances. In the traversal kernel, `best_t` now starts at `FLT_MAX`, the stack pop uses `stack[--sp]` instead of reading past the top, pruning compares directly against the current closest hit, and the leaf test is tightened to `is_leaf == 1`.

I verified it by building with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running the included harness. The result was `CPU_HITS=10`, `GPU_HITS=10`, `MISMATCHES=0`, `MISSED_HITS=0`, `FALSE_HITS=0`, `MATCH=1`.

Updated [src/md_neighbor.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_nucpay0c/src/md_neighbor.cu) so the GPU path matches the CPU reference.

In the [neighbor-list builder](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_nucpay0c/src/md_neighbor.cu#L50-L74), I changed the cutoff check to compare `r2` against `cutoff^2`, skipped `j == i` so self-interactions never enter the list, and indexed neighbors with stride `N` instead of a fixed 64-entry cap. That last change prevents silent truncation at larger cutoffs. In the [GPU LJ kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_nucpay0c/src/md_neighbor.cu#L80-L115), I restored the correct `sigma_r12 = sigma_r6 * sigma_r6` term and kept the force accumulation consistent with the CPU formula.

Verification: `nvcc src/md_neighbor.cu -o md_neighbor -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` succeeded, and `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_md_neighbor.py` passed with `9 passed`.
